Healthcare Feature Engineering¶

Objectives¶

It is important in any data science project to define the objective as specific as possible. Below let's write it from general to specific. This will direct your analysis.

  • Detect any hospital that may abuse the resources.
  • Detect any hospital that may abuse the resources compared to its peers.
  • Detect any hospital that may abuse the resources compared to the average (median etc) of its peers.
  • Detect any hospital that may abuse the resources compared to the average (median etc) of its peers of the same DRG and State.

FE:¶

  • We will focus on the "Average Medicare Payments", which is the "Medicare Payment Amount / Total Discharges" to create features.

Table of Contents¶

  • Section 1: data preparation
    • Section 1.1: Load data
    • Section 1.2: data preparation
  • Section 2: EDA
    • Section 2.1: Distribution plots
    • Section 2.2: Count by state(#Sect
    • Section 2.3: Count of top cities

    • Section 3.2: Create the benchmark table - State & DRG total discharge
    • Section 3.3: Create the benchmark table - State & DRG Average_Medicare_Payment
    • Section 3.4: Create the benchmark table - State & DRG Average Total Payment per discharge
    • Section 3.5: Create the benchmark table - State & DRG Average Medicare per discharge

Section 1: Data preparation ¶

Section 1.1: Load Data ¶

  • Import packages and data
  • Rename column
  • Change data type and transfer spending amount with dollar sign into numeric
  • Understand the data strcuture.
In [1]:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
%matplotlib inline
In [2]:
data = pd.read_csv('inpatientCharges.csv')
#data.head()
#data.describe
data.info()

In [3]:
data.columns = ['DRG','Provider_Id', 'Provider_Name','Provider_StreetAddress','Provider_City',
               'Provider_State','Provider_Zipcode','Hospital_referral_region_desp',
                'Total_Discharges','Average_Covered_Charges','Average_Total_Payments',
                'Average_Medicare_Payment']
data = data.drop('Average_Covered_Charges',axis=1)

In [5]:
# Change data type and transfer spending amount with dollar sign into numeric
data['DRG'] = data['DRG'].astype('category')
data['Provider_State'] = data['Provider_State'].astype('category')
data['Provider_City'] = data['Provider_City'].astype('category')
data['Provider_Id'] = data['Provider_Id'].astype('category')
data['Average_Total_Payments'] = (data['Average_Total_Payments']
                                  .str.replace(r'[$,]', '', regex=True)
                                  .astype(float))

data['Average_Medicare_Payment'] = (data['Average_Medicare_Payment']
                                    .str.replace(r'[$,]', '', regex=True)
                                    .astype(float))
In [6]:
data.info()

Section 3: Feature Engineering ¶

Discussions:¶

  • Think of the objective: "Detect any hospital that may abuse the resources compared to the average of its peers of the same DRG and State."
  • A feature should be able to "detect any hospital...". The above table does not have hospitals.
  • The above table is the benchmark. Are they features?

Section 3.1: Create the benchmark table - State & DRG average total payment ¶

For this benchmark¶

Feature 1: Ratio of a Hospital's "Average_Total_Payment" to the State Average_Total_Payment By DRG¶

Feature 2: Ratio of the "Average_Total_Payment" in a City to the State Average_Total_Payment By DRG¶

Feature 3: Ratio of a Provider's "Average_Total_Payment" to State Average_Total_Payment By DRG¶

In [13]:
#这段benchmark是计算了每个州（Provider_State）和每个DRG类别内的平均支付金额（Avg_Payment_ByStateDRG）。
#可以把每家医院的支付金额与它所在的州和DRG类别的平均值来进行对比，找出是否有医院明显超出平均值。
#Here is the benchmark
benchmark_by_stateDRG = data.groupby(['Provider_State','DRG'])['Average_Total_Payments'].mean().reset_index()
benchmark_by_stateDRG.columns = ['Provider_State','DRG','Avg_Payment_ByStateDRG']
benchmark_by_stateDRG.head()

/var/folders/b0/0hqxwf0x335bj2w1kt567mth0000gn/T/ipykernel_38640/2817759685.py:4: FutureWarning: The default of observed=False is deprecated and will be changed to True in a future version of pandas. Pass observed=False to retain current behavior or observed=True to adopt the future default and silence this warning.
  benchmark_by_stateDRG = data.groupby(['Provider_State','DRG'])['Average_Total_Payments'].mean().reset_index()
Out[13]:

# Merge this benchmark with dataset
F123 = pd.merge(data, benchmark_by_stateDRG, how='left', on=['Provider_State', 'DRG'])

163065 rows × 12 columns

Feature 1: Ratio of a Hospital's "Average_Total_Payment" to the State Average_Total_Payment By DRG¶

The goal of feature1 is to identify and detect anomalies in hospital biling practices by comparing the average total payment of an individual hospital to the state average for the same Diagnosis Related Group(DRG). This feature helps to highlight hospitals that might be in overbilling or underbilling.¶
In [15]:
#Create the Feature 1：Ratio of a Hospital's "Average_Total_Payment" to the State Average_Total_Payment By DRG
F123['Hospital_Average_Total_Payments_bySTDRG'] = F123['Average_Total_Payments'] / F123['Avg_Payment_ByStateDRG'].replace(0, np.nan)
In [16]:
plt.figure(figsize=(8, 4))
sns.histplot(data=F123, x='Hospital_Average_Total_Payments_bySTDRG', kde=True, bins=30)
plt.xlim(0, 2)
plt.title("Histogram and KDE of Hospital Average Payments", fontsize=14)
plt.xlabel("Hospital_Average_Total_Payments_bySTDRG", fontsize=12)

plt.show()

/opt/anaconda3/lib/python3.11/site-packages/seaborn/_oldcore.py:1119: FutureWarning: use_inf_as_na option is deprecated and will be removed in a future version. Convert inf values to NaN before operating instead.
  with pd.option_context('mode.use_inf_as_na', True):
Most hospitals in the dataset have a ratio between 0.5 and 1.5, which means their payments are relatively close to the state average for thir DRG category. And the KDE peak here around 1, showing that the majority of hospitals are charging approximately the same as the state average. And the plot is right-skewed, meaning that while most hospitals charge near the state average, there are a few hospitals that charge much more. The long tail on the right suggests there existing outliers.¶

Feature 2: Ratio of the "Average_Total_Payment" in a City to the State Average_Total_Payment By DRG¶

The goal of create the feature2 is to compare the average total payment across all hospitals within a city to the state average for the same DRG. This feature helps to identify regional billing trends and detect potential anomalies at the city level, revealing cities where healthcare costs might be unusually high or low compared to the statebench mark.¶
In [17]:
city_avg_payment = F123.groupby(['Provider_City', 'DRG'])['Average_Total_Payments'].mean().reset_index()
city_avg_payment.columns = ['Provider_City', 'DRG', 'City_Avg_Payment']

# Merge the city average payment with the main dataset
F123 = pd.merge(F123, city_avg_payment, on=['Provider_City', 'DRG'], how='left')

# Calculate Feature 2: Ratio of City Average Payment to State Average Payment by DRG
F123['City_to_State_Payment_Ratio'] = F123['City_Avg_Payment'] / F123['Avg_Payment_ByStateDRG']

/var/folders/b0/0hqxwf0x335bj2w1kt567mth0000gn/T/ipykernel_38640/4161074543.py:1: FutureWarning: The default of observed=False is deprecated and will be changed to True in a future version of pandas. Pass observed=False to retain current behavior or observed=True to adopt the future default and silence this warning.
  city_avg_payment = F123.groupby(['Provider_City', 'DRG'])['Average_Total_Payments'].mean().reset_index()
In [18]:
# Distribution of City Average Payments
plt.figure(figsize=(8, 4))
sns.histplot(data=F123, x='City_to_State_Payment_Ratio', kde=True, bins=30)
plt.xlim(0, 2)
plt.title("Histogram and KDE of City to State Payment Ratio", fontsize=14)
plt.xlabel("City_to_State_Payment_Ratio", fontsize=12)

plt.show()

/opt/anaconda3/lib/python3.11/site-packages/seaborn/_oldcore.py:1119: FutureWarning: use_inf_as_na option is deprecated and will be removed in a future version. Convert inf values to NaN before operating instead.
  with pd.option_context('mode.use_inf_as_na', True):
Most cities have a ratio close to 1, cities are billing Medicare payments similar to the state average for the same DRG. Cities with ratios lower than 1 are charging less than the state average. This could mean lower costs or more efficient billing practices. Most data points cluster here are around 0.75 to 1.25, the range shows small deviations around the state average. And a peak is close to 1, most cities align well with the state-level billing.¶

Feature 3: Ratio of a Provider's "Average_Total_Payment" to State Average_Total_Payment By DRG¶

The goal to create this feature3 is to evaluate and compare the average total payment of individual providers to the state average for the same DRG. It will detect outlier providers whose billing practices significantly deviate from the normal, identifying potential cases of overbilling, underbilling.¶
In [19]:
provider_avg_payment = data.groupby(['Provider_Id', 'DRG'])['Average_Total_Payments'].mean().reset_index()
provider_avg_payment.columns = ['Provider_Id', 'DRG', 'Provider_Avg_Payment']

# Merge the provider average payment with the main dataset
F123 = pd.merge(F123, provider_avg_payment, on=['Provider_Id', 'DRG'], how='left')

# Calculate Feature 3: Ratio of Provider Average Payment to State Average Payment by DRG
F123['Provider_to_State_Payment_Ratio'] = F123['Provider_Avg_Payment'] / F123['Avg_Payment_ByStateDRG']

/var/folders/b0/0hqxwf0x335bj2w1kt567mth0000gn/T/ipykernel_38640/1864718560.py:1: FutureWarning: The default of observed=False is deprecated and will be changed to True in a future version of pandas. Pass observed=False to retain current behavior or observed=True to adopt the future default and silence this warning.
  provider_avg_payment = data.groupby(['Provider_Id', 'DRG'])['Average_Total_Payments'].mean().reset_index()
In [20]:
# Plot the distribution of the new feature
plt.figure(figsize=(8, 4))
sns.histplot(data=F123, x='Provider_to_State_Payment_Ratio', kde=True, bins=30)
plt.xlim(0, 2)
plt.title("Histogram and KDE of Provider to State Payment Ratio", fontsize=14)
plt.xlabel("Provider_to_State_Payment_Ratio", fontsize=12)

plt.show()

/opt/anaconda3/lib/python3.11/site-packages/seaborn/_oldcore.py:1119: FutureWarning: use_inf_as_na option is deprecated and will be removed in a future version. Convert inf values to NaN before operating instead.
  with pd.option_context('mode.use_inf_as_na', True):
Most providers have a ratio close to 1, this suggests that the majority of providers charge medicare payments that are consistent with the state average for the same DRG, indicating standardized billing pratices. And providers with ratio above 1 are charging more than the state average, which could indicate higher service costs or potential overbilling.¶

Section 3.2: Create the benchmark table - State & DRG total discharge¶

Feature 4: Ratio of a Hospital's "Total Discharges" to the State Total Discharges By DRG¶

Feature 5: Ratio of the "Total Discharges" in a City to the State Total Discharges By DRG¶

Feature 6: Ratio of a Provider's "Total Discharges" to the State Total Discharges By DRG¶

In [21]:
# Create benchmark for State Total_Discharges by DRG
benchmark_discharges_by_stateDRG = data.groupby(['Provider_State', 'DRG'])['Total_Discharges'].sum().reset_index()
benchmark_discharges_by_stateDRG.columns = ['Provider_State', 'DRG', 'Total_Discharges_ByStateDRG']

# Merge
F345 = pd.merge(data, benchmark_discharges_by_stateDRG, how='left', on=['Provider_State', 'DRG'])

print("First few rows of the F345 dataset:")
F345.head()

First few rows of the F345 dataset:

Pass observed=False to retain current behavior or observed=True to adopt the future default and silence this warning.

Feature 4: Ratio of a Hospital's "Total Discharges" to the State Total Discharges By DRG¶

The goal to create this feature4 is to compare the total number of discharges from a specific hospital to the state-level total discharge for the same DRP. This feature to detect anomalies in how hospitals handle patient volumnes for particular medical procedures, allowing for insights into hospital capacity, efficiency, and potential overuse or underuse of resources.¶
In [22]:
# Calculate Feature 4: Ratio of a hospital's discharge to the state discharge by DRG
F345['Hospital_to_State_Discharge_Ratio'] = F345['Total_Discharges'] / F345['Total_Discharges_ByStateDRG']

# Plot the distribution of the new feature
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
sns.histplot(data=F345, x='Hospital_to_State_Discharge_Ratio', kde=True, bins=80)
plt.xlim(0, 0.5) 
plt.title("Histogram and KDE of Hospital to State Discharge Ratio", fontsize=14)
plt.xlabel("Hospital_to_State_Discharge_Ratio", fontsize=12)
plt.ylabel("Count", fontsize=12)

plt.show()

/opt/anaconda3/lib/python3.11/site-packages/seaborn/_oldcore.py:1119: FutureWarning: use_inf_as_na option is deprecated and will be removed in a future version. Convert inf values to NaN before operating instead.
  with pd.option_context('mode.use_inf_as_na', True):
Most hospitals have a very low ratio inficates that the majority of hospitals handle a small fraction of the state's total discharges for specific DRGs. The graph is heavily skewed to the right, showing that a few hospitals have higher ratios, meaning they manage more discharges than most others. Hospitals with ratios significantly higher than the rest which are in the long tail of the graph could be outliers indicating high patient volumes or potential overuse of service.¶

Feature 5: Ratio of the "Total Discharges" in a City to the State Total Discharges By DRG¶

This feature 5 is compare the total number of discharges from all hospitals within a city to the state-wide total discharges for the same DRG. The goal for this feature is to anlayze regional trends in patient volume, identify cities with unusual high or low service utilization, and provide insights into resource distribution and healthcare accessbility.¶
In [23]:
city_total_discharges = F345.groupby(['Provider_City', 'DRG'])['Total_Discharges'].sum().reset_index()
city_total_discharges.columns = ['Provider_City', 'DRG', 'City_Total_Discharges']

# Merge the city total discharges with the main dataset
F345 = pd.merge(F345, city_total_discharges, on=['Provider_City', 'DRG'], how='left')

# Calculate Feature 5
F345['City_to_State_Discharge_Ratio'] = F345['City_Total_Discharges'] / F345['Total_Discharges_ByStateDRG']

/var/folders/b0/0hqxwf0x335bj2w1kt567mth0000gn/T/ipykernel_38640/2300019175.py:1: FutureWarning: The default of observed=False is deprecated and will be changed to True in a future version of pandas. Pass observed=False to retain current behavior or observed=True to adopt the future default and silence this warning.
  city_total_discharges = F345.groupby(['Provider_City', 'DRG'])['Total_Discharges'].sum().reset_index()
In [24]:
# Plot the distribution of the new feature
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
sns.histplot(data=F345, x='City_to_State_Discharge_Ratio', kde=True, bins=120)
plt.xlim(0, 1)  
plt.title("Histogram and KDE of City to State Discharge Ratio", fontsize=14)
plt.xlabel("City_to_State_Discharge_Ratio", fontsize=12)
plt.ylabel("Count", fontsize=12)

plt.show()

/opt/anaconda3/lib/python3.11/site-packages/seaborn/_oldcore.py:1119: FutureWarning: use_inf_as_na option is deprecated and will be removed in a future version. Convert inf values to NaN before operating instead.
  with pd.option_context('mode.use_inf_as_na', True):
The graph is right-skewed showing that a few cities have higher ratios, they handle more discharges than others. And cities with ratios which are significantly higher than the rest, which are in long tail are outliers, suggesting that they may have specialized healthcare centers or higher demand for certain medical service, maybe requiring further analysis to understand reason.¶

Feature 6: Ratio of a Provider's "Total Discharges" to the State Total Discharges By DRG¶

The feature 6 compares the total number of discharges handled by an individual provider to the state-wide total discharge for the same DRG. The objective is to identify providers who play a significant role in handling specific types of medical case, to detect any anomalies in service utlization that could indicate overuse, underuse, or inefficiencies.¶
In [25]:
# Calculate Feature 6: Ratio of Provider's Discharge to State Discharge by DRG
F345['Provider_to_State_Discharge_Ratio'] = F345['Total_Discharges'] / F345['Total_Discharges_ByStateDRG']

# Plot the distribution of the new feature
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
sns.histplot(data=F345, x='Provider_to_State_Discharge_Ratio', kde=True, bins=30)
plt.xlim(0, 0.5)  
plt.title("Histogram and KDE of Provider to State Discharge Ratio", fontsize=14)
plt.xlabel("Provider_to_State_Discharge_Ratio", fontsize=12)
plt.ylabel("Count", fontsize=12)

plt.show()

/opt/anaconda3/lib/python3.11/site-packages/seaborn/_oldcore.py:1119: FutureWarning: use_inf_as_na option is deprecated and will be removed in a future version. Convert inf values to NaN before operating instead.
  with pd.option_context('mode.use_inf_as_na', True):
This graph is also right-skewed, with a few providers showing higher ratios, meaning they handle more discharges compared to others, and most providers contribute a small portion of the state's total discharges for specific DRGs. Providers with signifcantly higher ratios are outliers.¶

Section 3.3: Create the benchmark table - State & DRG Average_Medicare_Payment¶

Feature 7: Ratio of a Hospital's "Average Medicare Payment" to the State Average Medicare Payment By DRG¶

Feature 8: Ratio of the "Average Medicare Payment" in a City to the State Average Medicare Payment By DRG¶

Feature 9: Ratio of a Provider's "Average Medicare Payment" to the State Average Medicare Payment By DRG¶

In [26]:
# Create benchmark for State Average Medical Payment by DRG
benchmark_medical_payment_by_stateDRG = data.groupby(['Provider_State', 'DRG'])['Average_Medicare_Payment'].mean().reset_index()
benchmark_medical_payment_by_stateDRG.columns = ['Provider_State', 'DRG', 'Avg_Medicare_Payment_ByStateDRG']

print("New benchmark - State Average Medicare Payment by DRG:")
benchmark_medical_payment_by_stateDRG.head()

New benchmark - State Average Medicare Payment by DRG:

Feature 7: Ratio of a Hospital's "Average Medicare Payment" to the State Average Medicare Payment By DRG¶

The feature 7 compares the average medicare payment charged by a specific hospital to the state-wide average medicare payment for the smae DRG. The purpose of this feature is to identify variations in Medicare billing practices across hospitals, detect anomalies in pricing.¶
In [28]:
# Calculate Feature 7
F789['Hospital_to_State_Medicare_Payment_Ratio'] = F789['Average_Medicare_Payment'] / F789['Avg_Medicare_Payment_ByStateDRG']

# Plot the distribution
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
sns.histplot(data=F789, x='Hospital_to_State_Medicare_Payment_Ratio', kde=True, bins=30)
plt.xlim(0, 2) 
plt.title("Histogram and KDE of Hospital to State Medicare Payment Ratio", fontsize=14)
plt.xlabel("Hospital_to_State_Medicare_Payment_Ratio", fontsize=12)
plt.ylabel("Count", fontsize=12)

plt.show()

/opt/anaconda3/lib/python3.11/site-packages/seaborn/_oldcore.py:1119: FutureWarning: use_inf_as_na option is deprecated and will be removed in a future version. Convert inf values to NaN before operating instead.
  with pd.option_context('mode.use_inf_as_na', True):
Most hospitals have a ratio around 1, indicating that the majority of hospitals charge medicare payment close to the state average. And the graph shows a little right-skewed here, meaning there are hospitals with higher ratios charge more than the state average. For hospitals with ratios significantly greater than 1 are outliers, suggesting higher medicare payment compared to the state average, which may indicate potential overbilling or inefficiencies.¶

Feature 8: Ratio of the "Average Medicare Payment" in a City to the State Average Medicare Payment By DRG¶

The feature 8 compares the average medicare payment charged across all hospitals within a specific city to the state-wide average medicare payment for the smae DRG. The purpose for this feature is to identify regional variations in medicare billing, understand city-level pricing trends, and help ensure fair and consistent Medicare charges across different cities within a state.¶
In [29]:
# Calculate the average Medicare payment by city and DRG
city_avg_medicare_payment = F789.groupby(['Provider_City', 'DRG'])['Average_Medicare_Payment'].mean().reset_index()
city_avg_medicare_payment.columns = ['Provider_City', 'DRG', 'City_Avg_Medicare_Payment']

# Merge
F789 = pd.merge(F789, city_avg_medicare_payment, on=['Provider_City', 'DRG'], how='left')

# Calculate Feature 8
F789['City_to_State_Medicare_Payment_Ratio'] = F789['City_Avg_Medicare_Payment'] / F789['Avg_Medicare_Payment_ByStateDRG']

# Plot the distribution
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
sns.histplot(data=F789, x='City_to_State_Medicare_Payment_Ratio', kde=True, bins=30)
plt.xlim(0, 2) 
plt.title("Histogram and KDE of City to State Medicare Payment Ratio", fontsize=14)
plt.xlabel("City_to_State_Medicare_Payment_Ratio", fontsize=12)
plt.ylabel("Count", fontsize=12)

plt.show()

/var/folders/b0/0hqxwf0x335bj2w1kt567mth0000gn/T/ipykernel_38640/1806492429.py:2: FutureWarning: The default of observed=False is deprecated and will be changed to True in a future version of pandas. Pass observed=False to retain current behavior or observed=True to adopt the future default and silence this warning.
  city_avg_medicare_payment = F789.groupby(['Provider_City', 'DRG'])['Average_Medicare_Payment'].mean().reset_index()
/opt/anaconda3/lib/python3.11/site-packages/seaborn/_oldcore.py:1119: FutureWarning: use_inf_as_na option is deprecated and will be removed in a future version. Convert inf values to NaN before operating instead.
  with pd.option_context('mode.use_inf_as_na', True):
Most cities have a ratio around 1, suggesting that the majority of cities charge Medicare payments close to the state average, indicating consistenting billing practices across cities. The graph is almost symmetrical, with a peak around 1, meaning most cities' payments are near the state average. There are fewer cities on the right, indicating some cities charge higher than the average, Cities with ratios significantly greater than 1 are outliers that may indicate higher billing or overcharging compared the state norm.¶

Feature 9: Ratio of a Provider's "Average Medicare Payment" to the State Average Medicare Payment By DRG¶

The feature 9 compares the average medicare payment charged by an individual providers to the state-wide averafe medicare payment for the same DRG. The purpose of this feature is to identify outlier providers who deviate from typical billing practices, detecting potential overbilling, underbilling and inefficiencies at the provider level.¶
In [30]:
# Calculate Feature 9
F789['Provider_to_State_Medicare_Payment_Ratio'] = F789['Average_Medicare_Payment'] / F789['Avg_Medicare_Payment_ByStateDRG']

# Plot the distribution 
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
sns.histplot(data=F789, x='Provider_to_State_Medicare_Payment_Ratio', kde=True, bins=30)
plt.xlim(0, 2) 
plt.title("Histogram and KDE of Provider to State Medicare Payment Ratio", fontsize=14)
plt.xlabel("Provider_to_State_Medicare_Payment_Ratio", fontsize=12)
plt.ylabel("Count", fontsize=12)

plt.show()

/opt/anaconda3/lib/python3.11/site-packages/seaborn/_oldcore.py:1119: FutureWarning: use_inf_as_na option is deprecated and will be removed in a future version. Convert inf values to NaN before operating instead.
  with pd.option_context('mode.use_inf_as_na', True):
Most providers have a ratio around 1, indicating that the majority of providers charge Medicare payments close to the state average. And the distribution here is slightly right-skewed, there are some providers charging higher than the average. Providers with ratios significantly greater than 1 are outliers, that may indicate overbillinng or higher costs.¶

Section 3.4: Create the benchmark table - State & DRG Average Total Payment per discharge¶

Feature 10: Ratio of Hospital's Average Payment to State Average Total Payment per Discharge by DRG¶

Feature 11: Ratio of Average Payment by City to State Average Total Payment per Discharge by DRG¶

Feature 12: Ratio of Provider's Average Payment per Discharge to State Average Total Payment per Discharge by DRG¶

In [31]:
# Create benchmark for State Average Total Payment per Discharge by DRG
benchmark_payment_per_discharge_by_stateDRG = data.groupby(['Provider_State', 'DRG']).agg({
    'Average_Total_Payments': 'sum',
    'Total_Discharges': 'sum'
}).reset_index()

benchmark_payment_per_discharge_by_stateDRG['Avg_Payment_Per_Discharge_ByStateDRG'] = (
    benchmark_payment_per_discharge_by_stateDRG['Average_Total_Payments'] / 
    benchmark_payment_per_discharge_by_stateDRG['Total_Discharges']
)

benchmark_payment_per_discharge_by_stateDRG = benchmark_payment_per_discharge_by_stateDRG[['Provider_State', 'DRG', 'Avg_Payment_Per_Discharge_ByStateDRG']]
benchmark_payment_per_discharge_by_stateDRG.head()

# Merge this new benchmark with the original data dataset and name it F101112
F101112 = pd.merge(data, benchmark_payment_per_discharge_by_stateDRG, how='left', on=['Provider_State', 'DRG'])
F101112.head()

Pass observed=False to retain current behavior or observed=True to adopt the future default and silence this warning.

Feature 10: Ratio of Hospital's Average Payment to State Average Total Payment per Discharge by DRG¶

Feature 10 is to compare the average payment per discharge for a specific hospital to the state-wide average payment per discharge for the same DRG. This helps to identify variations in billing practices and potential cost efficiencies or inefficiencies.¶
In [32]:
# hospital's average payment per discharge
F101112['Hospital_Avg_Payment_Per_Discharge'] = F101112['Average_Total_Payments'] / F101112['Total_Discharges']

# Calculate Feature 10
F101112['Hospital_to_State_Payment_Per_Discharge_Ratio'] = F101112['Hospital_Avg_Payment_Per_Discharge'] / F101112['Avg_Payment_Per_Discharge_ByStateDRG']

# Plot the distribution of the new feature
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
sns.histplot(data=F101112, x='Hospital_to_State_Payment_Per_Discharge_Ratio', kde=True, bins=200)
plt.xlim(0, 2) 
plt.title("Histogram and KDE of Hospital to State Payment Per Discharge Ratio", fontsize=14)
plt.xlabel("Hospital_to_State_Payment_Per_Discharge_Ratio", fontsize=12)
plt.ylabel("Count", fontsize=12)

plt.show()

/opt/anaconda3/lib/python3.11/site-packages/seaborn/_oldcore.py:1119: FutureWarning: use_inf_as_na option is deprecated and will be removed in a future version. Convert inf values to NaN before operating instead.
  with pd.option_context('mode.use_inf_as_na', True):
Most hospitals have a ratio near 1, suggesting that average payment per discharge for most hospitals is close to the state average. And the graph is fairly balanced around1, there are hospitals that charge slightly more or less per discharge compared to the state average.¶

Feature 11: Ratio of Average Payment by City to State Average Total Payment per Discharge by DRG¶

The feature 11 indicate to compare the average payment per discharge at the city level to the state-wide average for the same DRG. This helps in identifying variations in healthcare costs across different cities.¶
In [33]:
# Calculate the average payment per discharge by city and DRG
city_avg_payment_per_discharge = F101112.groupby(['Provider_City', 'DRG']).agg({
    'Average_Total_Payments': 'sum',
    'Total_Discharges': 'sum'
}).reset_index()

city_avg_payment_per_discharge['City_Avg_Payment_Per_Discharge'] = (
    city_avg_payment_per_discharge['Average_Total_Payments'] / 
    city_avg_payment_per_discharge['Total_Discharges']
)
# Merge the city average payment per discharge with the origin dataset
F101112 = pd.merge(F101112, city_avg_payment_per_discharge[['Provider_City', 'DRG', 'City_Avg_Payment_Per_Discharge']], 
                   on=['Provider_City', 'DRG'], how='left')

Pass observed=False to retain current behavior or observed=True to adopt the future default and silence this warning.

# Plot the distribution
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
sns.histplot(data=F101112, x='City_to_State_Payment_Per_Discharge_Ratio', kde=True, bins=200)
plt.xlim(0, 2)

/opt/anaconda3/lib/python3.11/site-packages/seaborn/_oldcore.py:1119: FutureWarning: use_inf_as_na option is deprecated and will be removed in a future version. Convert inf values to NaN before operating instead.
  with pd.option_context('mode.use_inf_as_na', True):
The distribution is centered around 1, indicateing that most cities have average payments per discharge close to the state average. Here, outliers can be identified as values far below or above the peak which is 1.¶

Feature 12: Ratio of Provider's Average Payment per Discharge to State Average Total Payment per Discharge by DRG¶

This feature identifies potential discrepancies or inefficiencies at the provider level.¶
In [35]:
# Calculate Feature 12: Ratio of Provider's Average Payment per Discharge to State Average Payment per Discharge by DRG
F101112['Provider_Avg_Payment_Per_Discharge'] = F101112['Average_Total_Payments'] / F101112['Total_Discharges']
F101112['Provider_to_State_Payment_Per_Discharge_Ratio'] = F101112['Provider_Avg_Payment_Per_Discharge'] / F101112['Avg_Payment_Per_Discharge_ByStateDRG']

# Plot the distribution of the new feature
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
sns.histplot(data=F101112, x='Provider_to_State_Payment_Per_Discharge_Ratio', kde=True, bins=200)

# Set x-axis limits (adjust as needed based on your data)
plt.xlim(0, 2)  # You might need to adjust this based on your data distribution

# Add labels and title
plt.title("Histogram and KDE of Provider to State Payment Per Discharge Ratio", fontsize=14)
plt.xlabel("Provider_to_State_Payment_Per_Discharge_Ratio", fontsize=12)
plt.ylabel("Count", fontsize=12)

plt.show()

/opt/anaconda3/lib/python3.11/site-packages/seaborn/_oldcore.py:1119: FutureWarning: use_inf_as_na option is deprecated and will be removed in a future version. Convert inf values to NaN before operating instead.
  with pd.option_context('mode.use_inf_as_na', True):
Most values are clustered around1, indicating that many providers' payments per discharge are close to that state average. Outliers can be detected by looking for extreme values far from 1, especially those significantly higher like above 2.¶

Section 3.5: Create the benchmark table - State & DRG Average Medicare per discharge¶

Feature 13: Ratio of Hospital's Average Medicare Payment per discharge to State Average Payment per discharge by DRG¶

    'Average_Medicare_Payment': 'sum',
    'Total_Discharges': 'sum'
}).reset_index()

benchmark_medicare_per_discharge_by_stateDRG['Avg_Medicare_Per_Discharge_ByStateDRG'] = (
    benchmark_medicare_per_discharge_by_stateDRG['Average_Medicare_Payment'] /

)

benchmark_medicare_per_discharge_by_stateDRG = benchmark_medicare_per_discharge_by_stateDRG[['Provider_State', 'DRG', 'Avg_Medicare_Per_Discharge_ByStateDRG']]

Feature 13: Ratio of Hospital's Average Medicare Payment per discharge to State Average Payment per discharge by DRG¶

This feature helps to determine if a hospital's Medicare payments per discharge are significantly higher or lower compared to the state average within the same DRG.¶
In [37]:
# Calculate the hospital's average Medicare payment per discharge
F131415['Hospital_Avg_Medicare_Per_Discharge'] = F131415['Average_Medicare_Payment'] / F131415['Total_Discharges']

# Calculate Feature 13
F131415['Hospital_to_State_Medicare_Per_Discharge_Ratio'] = F131415['Hospital_Avg_Medicare_Per_Discharge'] / F131415['Avg_Medicare_Per_Discharge_ByStateDRG']

# Plot the distribution of the new feature
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
sns.histplot(data=F131415, x='Hospital_to_State_Medicare_Per_Discharge_Ratio', kde=True, bins=200)
plt.xlim(0, 2)

/opt/anaconda3/lib/python3.11/site-packages/seaborn/_oldcore.py:1119: FutureWarning: use_inf_as_na option is deprecated and will be removed in a future version. Convert inf values to NaN before operating instead.
  with pd.option_context('mode.use_inf_as_na', True):
The distribution for this feature centered around 1, indicating that most hospitals have payments close to the state average. Ratios significantly higher or lower than 1 may indicate anomalies.¶

Feature 14: Ratio of Average Medicare Payment by City per discharge to State Average Payment per discharge on DRG¶

This feature is to identify disparities at the city level. By comparing the average Medicare payment per discharge in a city to the state average within the same DRG, this feature helps to identify cost variations, highlighting cities where hospitals are charging significantly more or less per discharge compared to the state average.¶
In [38]:
# Calculate the average Medicare payment per discharge by city and DRG
city_avg_medicare_per_discharge = F131415.groupby(['Provider_City', 'DRG']).agg({
    'Average_Medicare_Payment': 'sum',
    'Total_Discharges': 'sum'
}).reset_index()

city_avg_medicare_per_discharge['City_Avg_Medicare_Per_Discharge'] = (
    city_avg_medicare_per_discharge['Average_Medicare_Payment'] / 
    city_avg_medicare_per_discharge['Total_Discharges']
)

# Merge with the main dataset
F131415 = pd.merge(F131415, city_avg_medicare_per_discharge[['Provider_City', 'DRG', 'City_Avg_Medicare_Per_Discharge']], 
                   on=['Provider_City', 'DRG'], how='left')

# Calculate Feature 14
F131415['City_to_State_Medicare_Per_Discharge_Ratio'] = F131415['City_Avg_Medicare_Per_Discharge'] / F131415['Avg_Medicare_Per_Discharge_ByStateDRG']

# Plot the distribution of the new feature
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
sns.histplot(data=F131415, x='City_to_State_Medicare_Per_Discharge_Ratio', kde=True, bins=200)
plt.xlim(0, 2) 
plt.title("Histogram and KDE of City to State Medicare Payment Per Discharge Ratio", fontsize=14)
plt.xlabel("City_to_State_Medicare_Per_Discharge_Ratio", fontsize=12)
plt.ylabel("Count", fontsize=12)

plt.show()

/opt/anaconda3/lib/python3.11/site-packages/seaborn/_oldcore.py:1119: FutureWarning: use_inf_as_na option is deprecated and will be removed in a future version. Convert inf values to NaN before operating instead.

Most values here are centered around 1, indicating that city-level payments per discharge are generally close to the state average. Extreme deviations like greater than 1.5 or less than 0.5 might be identified as outliers.¶

Feature 15: Ratio of Provider's Average Medicare Payment per discharge to State Average Payment per discharge by DRG¶

The feature 15 is to identify how individal providers' Medicare payment practices compare to the state benchmarks. Providers that consistently charge more than the state average for similar cases, which may indicate potential overbilling or inefficiencies. And providers that charge less than the state average, which indicate cost-saving practices or underbilling.¶
In [39]:
# Calculate Feature 15
F131415['Provider_Avg_Medicare_Per_Discharge'] = F131415['Average_Medicare_Payment'] / F131415['Total_Discharges']
F131415['Provider_to_State_Medicare_Per_Discharge_Ratio'] = F131415['Provider_Avg_Medicare_Per_Discharge'] / F131415['Avg_Medicare_Per_Discharge_ByStateDRG']

# Plot the distribution of the new feature
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
sns.histplot(data=F131415, x='Provider_to_State_Medicare_Per_Discharge_Ratio', kde=True, bins=200)
plt.xlim(0, 2) 
plt.title("Histogram and KDE of Provider to State Medicare Payment Per Discharge Ratio", fontsize=14)
plt.xlabel("Provider_to_State_Medicare_Per_Discharge_Ratio", fontsize=12)
plt.ylabel("Count", fontsize=12)

plt.show()

/opt/anaconda3/lib/python3.11/site-packages/seaborn/_oldcore.py:1119: FutureWarning: use_inf_as_na option is deprecated and will be removed in a future version. Convert inf values to NaN before operating instead.
  with pd.option_context('mode.use_inf_as_na', True):
It shows the distribution centered around 1, indicating the most providers' average Medicare payments are close to the state average. And outliers can be identified by observing values far from peak, especially on right side like ratios above 2, which may indicate the unusual high charges.¶
